answer:
\boxed{I}

The best control group should receive **no music** to isolate the effect of music on productivity, ensuring any observed differences are due to the experimental treatments (classical or light rock) rather than the presence of music itself. Other options introduce confounding variables (e.g., different genres, mixed schedules, or partial exposure), which would complicate the interpretation of results. This design adheres to the principle of a true control group in experimental research.
